cards.question: Page 8 in 'About this Guide' (General section) - detail 1: Explain 'About this Guide Intended audience M3' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: About this Guide Intended audience M3 Business Engine User Documentation provides guidance for end users and consultants regarding understanding basic concepts and using key processes in M3 Business Engine. Further information about the available programs and functionality is available as field help texts. Document structure M3 Business Engine User Documentation is a task-oriented documentation, providing descriptions on performing specific procedures, defining settings, and running specific, step-by-step procedures. To some extent, this documentation set also contains conceptual documents, providing background information or describing requirements and how they are matched in M3 Business Engine.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 8

cards.question: Page 8 in 'About this Guide' (General section) - detail 2: Explain 'This table provides a brief overview' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: This table provides a brief overview of the most common sections that appear in this document. Briefly describes what kind of information the document provides.Introduction Describes the consequence of a process completed or a concept run.Outcome Explains how the results can be used.Uses Describes, if applicable, any changes that have been implemented in M3. How the system is affected Describes the prerequisites of a process or a concept.Before you start Lists all relevant parameters with a detailed explanation.Parameters to set Describes, if applicable, the concept or the purpose of the concept and when and how it is run. Description Provides an overview (often as a flow chart) of the activities in the pro- cess.

cards.question: Page 10 in 'Budgeting' (Activity-Based Budgeting) - detail 1: Explain 'Chapter 1: Budgeting Account Entry Instructions' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Chapter 1: Budgeting Account Entry Instructions Account entry instructions are properties assigned to a pay element so that its transactions receive the correct account entry. The account entry can be defined for the pay element on the H panel in 'Pay Element. Open' (PRS040). It can be specified manually during entry, retrieved from the employee file, retrieved from other transactions, etc. Regardless of the method, any control codes indicating where the account entry will be retrieved from must always be activated for the pay element. For more details about account entry, see the document listed in the See also section. Description Pay transactions need to be recorded when accounting transactions must be generated for the general ledger. Activity-Based Budgeting This process is used to create a budget based on planned activities.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 10

cards.question: Page 10 in 'Budgeting' (Activity-Based Budgeting) - detail 2: Explain 'This includes the required resources and' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: This includes the required resources and related costs for each activity. The activities are a part of an activity plan. An activity plan is the basis for a resource plan that shows the resources required to carry out the activities. Resource plans also show available resources, so these can be compared to the required resources. Before you start • Your company must have a well-defined policy for how to handle and process budgeting. • Activities must be entered in ‘Budget Activity. Open’ (BUS010). • Resources must be entered in ‘Budget Resource.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 10

cards.question: Page 10 in 'Budgeting' (Activity-Based Budgeting) - detail 3: Explain 'Open’ (BUS015). • Costs must be' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open’ (BUS015). • Costs must be entered for each resource. More than one cost can be specified for each resource. Each cost consists of the price of the cost, the accounting identity to be used and, if necessary, an accounting curve. The costs for resources are entered in ‘Budg Resource. Connect Cost Components’ (BUS016), which is reached by using option 11=‘Cost component’ in (BUS015). M3 Business Controlling User Guide | Budgeting

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 10

cards.question: Page 11 in 'Budgeting' (Activity-Based Budgeting) - detail 1: Explain '• The resources required to carry' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • The resources required to carry out an activity must be entered for each activity. This is done in ‘Budget Activity. Connect Budg Resources’ (BUS011), which is reached by using option 11=‘Conn resources’ in (BUS010). • Budget officers must be entered. This is due to the fact that all activities are entered per budget officer. For each budget officer, information on accounting dimension to is specified. The information is used when transferring the resource plan to a budget. Budget officers are entered in ‘Budget Officer. Open’ (BUS035). • The available resources must be entered, indicating the scheduled resources available for each budget officer and period. This is done in ‘Budget Officer.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 11

cards.question: Page 11 in 'Budgeting' (Activity-Based Budgeting) - detail 2: Explain 'Connect Budg Resources’ (BUS036), which is' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Connect Budg Resources’ (BUS036), which is reached by using option 11=‘Avail resources’ in (BUS035). • A central budget with basic data must be entered in ‘Budget. Open’ (BUS100). Outcome The resource plan is transferred to a central budget after using this process. During the transfer, the number of activities multiplied by the number of available or required resources is recorded and made into budget transactions. Follow these steps Enter activities in activity plan Transfer activity plan to resource plan Adjust resource plan Transfer resource plan to budget Enter Activities in Activity Plan For each budget officer, budget number, version and period, you enter activities in ‘Budget. Connect Budget Activity’ (BUS160). The entered information makes up the activity plan, which can be printed for review. The plan can also be changed when necessary. Transfer Activity Plan to Resource Plan The activities in the plan are then multiplied by t

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 11

cards.question: Page 11 in 'Budgeting' (Activity-Based Budgeting) - detail 3: Explain 'Connect Budget Activity’ (BUS160). This results' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Connect Budget Activity’ (BUS160). This results in a resource plan. Adjust Resource Plan The resource plan contains the resources required according to the activity plan as well as the available resources entered for each budget officer. Therefore, this shows whether the budget officer has too many or too few resources to carry out the activities. Resources can then be added or deleted as necessary in ‘Budget. Connect Budget Resource’ (BUS170). Transfer Resource Plan to Budget After adjusting the resource plan you can use it to create budget transactions. This is done in (BUS170). The number of each resource in the plan is multiplied by the costs connected to the resource and a budget transaction is created. Note that the greater value of the number of resources or available resources is used in the calculation. M3 Business Controlling User Guide | Budgeting

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 11

cards.question: Page 12 in 'Budgeting' (Adjust Resource Plan) - detail 1: Explain 'Description The example below describes activity-based' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Description The example below describes activity-based budgeting, which includes creating an activity plan, resource plan and budget. Assume a budget officer must carry out 2, customer visits. Each visit takes hours of a sales resource and hours of a sales assistant resource. In this case, the activity plan will contain 2, customer visits. The resource plan will contain 16, hours of sales resources and 4, hours of sales assistant resources.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 12

cards.question: Page 12 in 'Budgeting' (Adjust Resource Plan) - detail 2: Explain 'The following average yearly and hourly' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The following average yearly and hourly costs are related to a salesperson, based on 1, hours worked annually. Hourly CostAnnual Cost 284.09500,000Salary 90.91160,000Social Security 28.4150,000Travel 14.2025,000Other The sales assistant has the following costs: Hourly CostAnnual Cost 170.45300,000Salary 54.5596,000Social Security The budget for this example has the following cost components: 16, x 284. + 4, x 170.45=5,227,240Salary 16, x 90. + 4, x 54.55=1,672,760Social Security 16, x 28.41=454,560Travel 16, x 14.20=227,200Other Adjust Resource Plan This procedure is used to adjust a resource plan after it is created. In this way, the number of resources calculated from the activity plan can be increased or decreased.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 12

cards.question: Page 12 in 'Budgeting' (Adjust Resource Plan) - detail 3: Explain 'Once reviewed and, if necessary, adjusted' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Once reviewed and, if necessary, adjusted, the plan is ready to be converted to a budget. Before You Start To start this procedure, a resource plan must be created. Refer to Create Resource Plan [Procedure]. M3 Business Controlling User Guide | Budgeting

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 12

cards.question: Page 13 in 'Budgeting' (Automatic Budgeting) - detail 1: Explain 'Follow These Steps Start ‘Budget. Connect' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Follow These Steps Start ‘Budget. Connect Budget Resource’ (BUS170/B). Check that the panel sequence is set to E1. Select option Open for the resource plan. On the E panel, enter or change the name and description of the resource plan. Press Enter to start ‘Budget. Enter Budget Resources’’ (BUS171/B). Review the calculated resources that are displayed for the plan in (BUS171/B). For each type of resource, the following information is displayed: • Required quantity • Available quantity • Adjustments in quantity • Total quantity, expressed as the sum of required quantity and adjusted quantity.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 13

cards.question: Page 13 in 'Budgeting' (Automatic Budgeting) - detail 2: Explain 'If necessary, adjust the quantity in' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: If necessary, adjust the quantity in the ‘Adjustment of resource quantity’ field and press Enter. The total quantity is then automatically recalculated. This can, for example, be done when the required quantity and the available quantity differ. Example: Required quantity is 500, available quantity and total quantity 500. By specifying –100, the total quantity is recalculated to ( – 100). Repeat the procedure from step until all resources are adjusted. Press F3. Automatic Budgeting This process is used to create a new budget from previous budgets and actual results. This is done by adding actual results to a specific period and budget to the later periods.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 13

cards.question: Page 13 in 'Budgeting' (Automatic Budgeting) - detail 3: Explain 'This addition results in an estimate' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: This addition results in an estimate for the year, which can than be adjusted up or down by a percentage. The budgets created in this way can be either final budgets or used as the basis for adjustments to the budgets of each budget officer. Before you start • The actual result for a specific period is recorded. • A budget is entered for the remaining periods. • Budget calculation templates are entered. Refer to Create Budget Calculation Templates [Procedures]. Follow these steps Select budget as basis for calculation Calculate new budget Update new budget Base for adjusting budget Select Budget as Basis for Calculation M3 Business Controlling User Guide | Budgeting

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 13

cards.question: Page 14 in 'Budgeting' (Automatic Reallocation) - detail 1: Explain 'This activity involves selecting the budget' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: This activity involves selecting the budget used as a basis for the calculation in ‘Budget. Create for Next Year’ (BUS120). Calculate New Budget This activity involves calculating new values from each element in the budget calculation template in ‘Budget. Create for Next Year’ (BUS120). Each element contains the following information: • The account entries to be selected • Where these entries should be stored • The percentage for calculating the estimate for the year • Whether trend calculations are made • The period accounting curve for the new budget. The account entries expected to be included in the calculation must be connected to an element, since only these connected account entries will be selected for the budget calculation. The account entries are stored in the accounting string used to update the new budget.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 14

cards.question: Page 14 in 'Budgeting' (Automatic Reallocation) - detail 2: Explain 'In this way, several accounts can' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: In this way, several accounts can be totaled into a different account. Trend calculation is when the newly budgeted totals (added to the last periods) are adjusted using the same ratio as actual against the original budget during the previous periods in the budget year. For example, if actual for the first nine periods has been percent of that budgeted, then actual for the last periods is also likely to be percent of that budgeted. Update New Budget The final activity involves updating the new budget with the calculated values in (BUS120). Basis for Adjusting Budget The new budget can be used as the basis for revising the budget from each budget officer. This depends on the report layout selected to print the new budget in (BUS120). When this is done, the budget is sent to each officer to be supplemented or approved.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 14

cards.question: Page 14 in 'Budgeting' (Automatic Reallocation) - detail 3: Explain 'Automatic Reallocation This process is used' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Automatic Reallocation This process is used to automatically reallocate a budget when the period accounting curve that applies for the budget is changed. The accounting curve is used to allocate the budget values to the periods in the budget. Before you start A budget with budget transactions must be entered in ‘Budget. Open’ (BUS100). A decision to change allocation curve must be made. Follow these steps Change period accounting curve Reallocation M3 Business Controlling User Guide | Budgeting

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 14

cards.question: Page 15 in 'Budgeting' (Cost control by commitment monitoring) - detail 1: Explain 'Change Period Accounting Curve Period accounting' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Change Period Accounting Curve Period accounting curves are changed in ‘Period Accounting Curve. Open’ (CRS450). The percentages or shares for each period can be changed. That means that any later entries will be processed according to the changed values. However, this does not change any previous entries. Reallocation Reallocation is done in ‘Budget. Reallocate’ (BUS130). This changes the previous budget entries.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 15

cards.question: Page 15 in 'Budgeting' (Cost control by commitment monitoring) - detail 2: Explain 'You reach (BUS130) by selecting option' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: You reach (BUS130) by selecting option 13=‘Chg budg allo’ for the budget you want to reallocate. In (BUS130), you specify the accounting curves that have been changed and press Enter. First, the detail transactions in a balance for each accounting dimension, currency code and allocation curve are deleted. The new transactions are then created according to the changes in the period accounting curve. Cost control by commitment monitoring Use this process to monitor a company’s purchase commitments, also known as purchase budgeting. Outcome • You have constant access to information about the current budget situation compared to the actual and committed expenditures per cost center or department. • Use this information to evaluate progress toward financial goals. • The actual values used in the calculation are saved in local currency in the FBAVAL file.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 15

cards.question: Page 15 in 'Budgeting' (Cost control by commitment monitoring) - detail 3: Explain '• The MPLINC file is updated' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • The MPLINC file is updated with the value of purchase order (PO) lines with order line status greater than or equal to that are included in the budget reserved for committed expenditures. Before you start Financial Management and General Management must be enabled and configured. Purpose The purpose of commitment monitoring is to help controlling overhead and capital expenditure. It applies for non-inventory control; that is, services, not goods. One example is marketing expenses. When applied Commitment monitoring is a continuous process. Workflow Commitment monitoring is made possible by including the value of goods ordered but not yet invoiced by the supplier as a cost in your cost follow-up. M3 Business Controlling User Guide | Budgeting

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 15

cards.question: Page 16 in 'Budgeting' (Cost control by commitment monitoring) - detail 1: Explain 'The traditional expenditure control consists of' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The traditional expenditure control consists of calculating the difference between budgeted expenditures and accumulated, actual expenditures on a regular basis: DifferenceActual year-to-date expen- ditures Budgeted expendituresCost center +17,00053,00070,000AAA +23,00027,00050,000BBB +2,00018,00020,000CCC +42,00098,000140,000Total In this example, the financials appear to be within budget, and all expenditures are reported as under control. However, if the value of all open PO lines is treated as committed expenditure, the financial picture may change significantly: DifferenceCommitmentsActual year-to-date Expenditures Budgeted expendi- tures Cost center 017,00053,00070,000AAA +400019,00027,00050,000BBB -6,0008,00018,00020,000CCC -2,00044,00098,000140,000Total With this information available, the company is able to act at an early stage to prevent such budget deficits. Enabling commitment monitoring Start by creating these budgets in '

cards.question: Page 16 in 'Budgeting' (Cost control by commitment monitoring) - detail 2: Explain '• One budget for manual entry' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • One budget for manual entry of overspending, but only if additional costs are approved. Then, in 'Settings – General Ledger' (CRS750/G), activate the calculation of committed expenditures and specify these parameters: • Which budgets to use. • Rules for comparing and down-dating (reducing) commitment values. • Which accounting dimensions to check during purchase order entry for cost control. • Choose whether to block a user from specifying a purchase order (PO) when no budget margin is available. Set parameter in 'Purchase Order Type.

cards.question: Page 16 in 'Budgeting' (Cost control by commitment monitoring) - detail 3: Explain 'Open' (PPS095) to either 1='Not stock'' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open' (PPS095) to either 1='Not stock' or 2='Yes' to enforce manual entry of the accounting string in 'Planned Purchase Order. Open' (PPS170) or 'Purchase Order. Open' (PPS201). This setting ensures that the accounting string is captured correctly for commitment monitoring and aligns with the control budget. M3 Business Controlling User Guide | Budgeting

cards.question: Page 17 in 'Budgeting' (Create Budget from Decentralized Budget) - detail 1: Explain 'Updating budgets The control budget is' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Updating budgets The control budget is updated using standard methods such as budget value generation, decentralized budgeting, allocations, and other approved processes. The commitment budget is updated overnight with committed values by M3 Job Scheduler. Old values are thus continuously replaced by new values. If needed, you can trigger the update manually for a period in 'Commitment. Calculate' (PPS970). Committed values are posted based on accounting rules PP10- and PP10-910. However, if you manually specify an accounting string by pressing F11 in any of these programs, the manual entry overrides those rules: • 'Purchase Requisition. Open' (PPS180) • 'Planned Purchase Order. Open' (PPS170) • 'Purchase Order.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 17

cards.question: Page 17 in 'Budgeting' (Create Budget from Decentralized Budget) - detail 2: Explain 'Open' (PPS200). Only the accounting dimensions' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open' (PPS200). Only the accounting dimensions selected for cost control in (CRS750/G) are used for the account entry. Online commitment control Commitment monitoring begins when a department specifies a purchase requisition or a purchase order line for a non-inventory item. If the accounting string is included in the control budget and is manually specified using F11, unless it is enforced by the purchase order type, the system performs this calculation automatically: (Budgeted value) plus (Manually entered overspend) minus (Actual year-to-date value for the accounting string) minus (Committed value) If manually specified overspending is available, the exchange rate type is retrieved from the budget. Otherwise, it is retrieved from the control budget. If the calculated amount exceeds the purchase order (PO) line amount, the excess is added to the general ledger balance file for the budget reserved for committed values. If the PO line i

cards.question: Page 17 in 'Budgeting' (Create Budget from Decentralized Budget) - detail 3: Explain 'Display results online By including the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Display results online By including the budgets used for commitment monitoring in a column template, you can display the current situation online for the affected cost centers or departments in 'GL Balance File. Display' (GLS215). Create Budget from Decentralized Budget This procedure is used to start a conversion of the information in a decentralized budget to account entries (that is, budget transactions) in a central budget. After using this procedure, ‘Decentralized Budget. Transfer’ (BUS119) is started and automatically processes the conversion. For information about how this is done, refer to Decentralized Budget. M3 Business Controlling User Guide | Budgeting

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 17

cards.question: Page 18 in 'Budgeting' (Create Budget Calculation Template) - detail 1: Explain 'Before you start • To start' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Before you start • To start this procedure, a decentralized budget must be entered. Refer to Enter Decentralized Budget [Procedure]. The decentralized budget must have general status 0=‘Not transferred’ and working status 0=‘Not blocked’. Follow these steps Start ‘Budget. Open Decentralized’ (BUS110/B). Select option 9=‘Transfer’ for the decentralized budget to create a budget. Confirm the conversion on the G panel by pressing Enter. Create Budget Calculation Template This procedure is used to define the template(s) used for generating budgets automatically. The templates consist of the information that controls how the new budgets are calculated. Before you start • Your company must have a well-defined policy for how to handle budgeting. • Period accounting curves must be defined in ‘Period Accounting Curve.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 18

cards.question: Page 18 in 'Budgeting' (Create Budget Calculation Template) - detail 2: Explain 'Open’ (CRS450). They are used to' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open’ (CRS450). They are used to automatically allocate values to the selected periods. Follow these steps Start ‘Budget Calculation Template. Open’ (BUS001/B). Check that the panel sequence is set to E1. Specify the ID of the new template and select option New. On the E panel, enter a name and description for the template, and the number of periods that the budgets created with the template should consist of. Press Enter to start ‘Budget Calc Template. Connect Elements’ (BUS002/B). In (BUS002/B), specify an ID for a budget template element and select option New. On the E panel, enter the percentages with which a budgeted amount and quantity should be adjusted, whether trend adjustment should be performed, the accounting curve to be used and the accounting identities to to be used.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 18

cards.question: Page 18 in 'Budgeting' (Create Budget Calculation Template) - detail 3: Explain 'Press Enter to start ‘Budg Calc' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Press Enter to start ‘Budg Calc Template. Connect Element Lines’ (BUS003/B). In (BUS003/B), specify the range of accounting strings to connect to the element. You do this by entering a line number, selecting option New and then specifying the range of accounting strings on the E panel. If necessary, you can create several lines; that is, several ranges for the same element. Press Enter and F3. Return to step if you want to create more elements for the budget template. Otherwise, press F3. M3 Business Controlling User Guide | Budgeting

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 18

cards.question: Page 19 in 'Budgeting' (Create Resource Plan) - detail 1: Explain 'Create Budget from Resource Plan This' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Create Budget from Resource Plan This procedure is used to transfer resources in a resource plan to account entries (that is, budget transactions) for a budget. These entries are created from the cost components connected to each resource. The account entries are then automatically transferred to a central budget. The greater value of planned quantity or adjusted quantity is used to calculate the costs. Before you start • A resource plan must be created. Refer to Create Resource Plan [Procedure]. • Costs per resource must be entered. This is done in ‘Budg Resource. Connect Cost Components’ (BUS016), which is reached by using option 11=‘Cost component’ in ‘Budget Resource. Open’ (BUS015).

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 19

cards.question: Page 19 in 'Budgeting' (Create Resource Plan) - detail 2: Explain '• The resource plan that is' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • The resource plan that is to be converted to a budget must have general status 0=‘Not transferred to a budget’ and working status 0=‘Not blocked’. Follow these steps Start ‘Budget. Connect Budget Resource’ (BUS170/B). Select option 9=‘Transfer’ for the resource plan to create a budget. On the G panel, confirm the transfer by pressing Enter. Create Resource Plan This procedure is used to create a resource plan using previously entered activities. Since each activity requires a number of resources, the resource plan contains the type and quantity of resources needed. Planned activities entered for a budget officer, budget number and budget version are converted into a resource plan after using this procedure. Before you start • All prerequisites specified in Activity-Based Budgeting on page must be met. • Activities must be entered per budget officer.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 19

cards.question: Page 19 in 'Budgeting' (Create Resource Plan) - detail 3: Explain 'Refer to Enter Activity for Budget' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Refer to Enter Activity for Budget Officer on page 23. • Resources must be entered per activity, indicating the number of resources required to carry out the activity. This is done in 'Budget Activity. Connect Budg Resources' (BUS011), which is reached by using option 11='Conn resources' in 'Budget Activity. Open' (BUS010). • The decentralized budget that is to be converted to a resource plan must have general status 0='Not transferred to a resource plan' and work status 0='Not blocked'. Follow these steps Start 'Budget. Connect Budget Activity' (BUS160/B). Select option 9='Transfer' for the budget to create a resource plan. M3 Business Controlling User Guide | Budgeting

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 19

cards.question: Page 20 in 'Budgeting' (Currency Simulation) - detail 1: Explain 'On the G panel, confirm the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: On the G panel, confirm the conversion by pressing Enter. Create Transfer Template This procedure is used to define standard settings for transferring documents from M3 to a PC and vice versa. The documents include budget transactions. The settings controls which budget transactions are to be included in the transfer by a range of accounting dimensions and the name of the PC document. You create templates per budget number and version. Before you start • A budget with basic data must be defined in ‘Budget. Open’ (BUS100) • The system administrator must create a folder on the M3 application server where the document can be stored. He/she must also specify the path to this folder in the ‘M3.properties’ file, which is also stored on the M3 application server.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 20

cards.question: Page 20 in 'Budgeting' (Currency Simulation) - detail 2: Explain 'Note that these prerequisites are Java' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Note that these prerequisites are Java specific. Follow these steps Start ‘Budget. Open’ (BUS100/B). Select option 17=‘Trf to/from PC’ for the budget for which you want to create a template to start ‘Budget. Import/Export PC File’ (BUS150/B). In (BUS150/B), specify an identity for the template and select option New. On the E panel, specify a name of the template, a range of accounting strings defining which budget transactions are to be copied when using this template, and the name of the PC document. Press Enter.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 20

cards.question: Page 20 in 'Budgeting' (Currency Simulation) - detail 3: Explain 'Return to step to create more' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Return to step to create more templates for the budget. Otherwise, press F3. Currency Simulation This process is used to simulate a budget to reflect changed exchange rates in order to determine the effect on budgeted actual results. You can chose too: • Update an existing budget to include changes in exchange rates • Create a new budget based on another, but recalculated in respect to the currencies. Before you start • A budget containing transactions in a foreign currency must be entered in 'Budget. Open' (BUS100) M3 Business Controlling User Guide | Budgeting

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 20

cards.question: Page 21 in 'Budgeting' (Currency Simulation) - detail 1: Explain '• If you want to create' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • If you want to create a new budget, this new budget must be defined with basic data in 'Budget. Open' (BUS100). Follow these steps Update exchange rates Start currency simulation Simulate Update current budget or create new one Compare budget actual Update Exchange Rates New exchange rates must be specified for the currency codes to be simulated. This can be done in the exchange rate type used to enter the budget or in another type. Note that the simulation is done one period at a time, so the exchange rates can be specified per period. This is done in 'Exchange Rate Type. Open' (CRS056). Start Currency Simulation This activity is done in 'Budget.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 21

cards.question: Page 21 in 'Budgeting' (Currency Simulation) - detail 2: Explain 'Simulate Currency/Exchange Rate' (BUS140), which is' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Simulate Currency/Exchange Rate' (BUS140), which is reached by using option 14='Currency sim' in 'Budget. Open' (BUS100). To start the simulation, the appropriate budget and range of currency codes are specified. If you want to create a new budget, specify the new budget's number and version as well. A budget can be simulated for one currency code at a time to determine the effect caused by changes to each currency. Simulate The calculations for the simulation are made in (BUS140) by using option 9='Run'. If you do not want to automatically update the current budget or create a new budget, but rather only want to review the simulation, make sure you do not mark the Update field on the E panel. The currency amount on a budget line is recalculated using the new exchange rate and compared to the amount in local currency on that budget line.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 21

cards.question: Page 21 in 'Budgeting' (Currency Simulation) - detail 3: Explain 'Assume the budgeted costs in USD' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Assume the budgeted costs in USD are as follows: USD 10, SEK 75, The new exchange rate for USD is set at 6.85. This results in the following changes: USD 10, SEK 68,500—a difference of SEK 6,500. Update Current Budget or Create New One This activity involves changing the current budget stated in the local currency. You do this in (BUS140) by using option 9='Run' and selecting the Update field on the E panel. The foreign currency is not affected. When updated, the amounts in the local currency in the new budget are those calculated as above, while the totals in the foreign currency remain the same as in the original budget. Compare Budget Actual M3 Business Controlling User Guide | Budgeting

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 21

cards.question: Page 22 in 'Budgeting' (Decentralized Budgeting) - detail 1: Explain 'The actual results in the new' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The actual results in the new budgets can be used to compare the differences between the original budget and simulated budgets in the report generator by using 'Report. Open' (RGS600). In this way, the original budget can be compared to a budget based on more pessimistic exchange rate fluctuations or on more positive ones. Decentralized Budgeting This process is used to enter a budget in a decentralized management environment. This allows each budget officer to control his/her budgeting. The process is completed when the regular budget is updated with the entries made by each budget officer. Before you start • Your company must have a well-defined routine for how to handle budgets. • Budget templates must be defined in ‘Budget Template. Open’ (BUS030).

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 22

cards.question: Page 22 in 'Budgeting' (Decentralized Budgeting) - detail 2: Explain 'Refer to Enter Budget Template [Procedure]' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Refer to Enter Budget Template [Procedure]. • Budget officers must be defined in ‘Budget Officer. Open’ (BUS035). Each budget officer contains information about, for example, the budget template used, the values used to calculate the yearly estimate and the accounting dimensions to to be used. • A budget with basic data must be defined in ‘Budget. Open’ (BUS100). Follow these steps Update budget officer Enter decentralized budget Create budget from decentralized budget Update Budget Officer To display the correct estimates, every budget officer must be updated to reflect the period to be used for calculations of the estimate for the current year. The update is done for each budget officer in ‘Budget Officer. Open’ (BUS035), usually by the senior officer in charge of the entire budget.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 22

cards.question: Page 22 in 'Budgeting' (Decentralized Budgeting) - detail 3: Explain 'The data that needs to be' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The data that needs to be updated includes: • The budget from which the calculation is made • The period for actual and budget. Enter Decentralized Budget The decentralized budget is entered by the budget officers themselves. First, each officer selects central budget and budget version. Then each officer enters amounts to be budgeted and/or resources to be budgeted and also how to allocate. The officer uses a budget template when doing this. Decentralized budget is entered in ‘Budget. Open Decentralized’ (BUS110). Create Budget from Decentralized Budget M3 Business Controlling User Guide | Budgeting

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 22

cards.question: Page 23 in 'Budgeting' (Enter Budget) - detail 1: Explain 'The information entered in the decentralized' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The information entered in the decentralized budget is transferred to the budget in ‘Budget. Open Decentralized’ (BUS110), by using option 9=‘Transfer’. This option starts ‘Decentralized Budget. Transfer’ (BUS119), which processes the actual transfer. The transfer is carried out as follows: 1. Budget lines in the decentralized budget containing an amount are recorded using accounting dimension from the budget template and dimensions to from the budget officer. 2. Budget lines in the decentralized budget containing a number of resources are recorded as follows: The cost(s) that are connected to each resource is retrieved. Accounting dimension is connected to that cost. The cost is then multiplied by the number of resources and a result is received.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 23

cards.question: Page 23 in 'Budgeting' (Enter Budget) - detail 2: Explain 'This result is recorded using accounting' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: This result is recorded using accounting dimension from the cost and dimensions to from the budget officer. An exception to step above is when the resource used belongs to a different cost center than the one specified for the budget officer. When this occurs, in addition to the above, a revenue record is entered for that cost center. Enter Activity for Budget Officer This procedure is used to enter planned activities during the budgeting period. Activities are entered for each budget officer and budget (budget number and version). Before you start • All prerequisites specified in Activity-Based Budgeting on page must be met. • You must know what activities are planned during the budgeting period. Follow these steps Start 'Budget. Connect Budget Activity' (BUS160/B). Check that the panel sequence is set to E1.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 23

cards.question: Page 23 in 'Budgeting' (Enter Budget) - detail 3: Explain 'Specify the budget officer, budget number' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Specify the budget officer, budget number and budget version to create a new budget and select option New. Enter a name and description on the E panel for this combination. Press Enter. Specify the activity and the planned number of resources required to carry out the activity plan during the budget period. Repeat step until all the activities are entered. Press Enter and F3. Enter Budget This procedure is used to enter budget transactions and allocate them to a budget. It can also be used to supplement and change current budgets. M3 Business Controlling User Guide | Budgeting

Open’ (CRS450). They are used to automatically allocate values to the selected periods. Follow These Steps Select ‘Budget. Open’ (BUS100/B). Specify option 11=‘Budget values’ to work with transactions in a budget. In ‘Budget. Update Periodic Budget Values’ (BUS101/B), check that the panel sequence is set to E1. Specify whether you want to budget amount, quantity or both in the ‘Amount/quantity’ field. Select one of the following alternatives: • To enter a new budget transa

cards.question: Page 24 in 'Budgeting' (Enter Budget) - detail 2: Explain '• To change an existing budget' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • To change an existing budget transaction and, if needed, reallocate it over the budget periods, go to step 9. • To allocate an existing budget transaction over days in a budget period, go to step 15. Enter a New Budget Transaction In (BUS101/B), enter a budget transaction by specifying the currency code, accounting string and allocation curve and select option New. On the E panel, a line for each period in the budget is displayed. Specify the transaction amount and/or quantity. Specify the curve number to use as well. Note that curve allocates the specified total amount and/or quantity evenly to each period, while allocates the same amount/quantity to each period and curve. Press Enter. The budget is displayed. Press Enter to confirm and return to step 5. Change an Existing Budget Transaction In (BUS101/B), select option Open for the budget transaction. Review the information displayed on the E panel. If necessary, change the appropri

cards.question: Page 24 in 'Budgeting' (Enter Budget) - detail 3: Explain 'The total entered is allocated in' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The total entered is allocated in accordance to the curve chosen. Press Enter and return to step 5. If necessary, change the period amount and/or quantity values on the appropriate line(s) and Press Enter. The new total is recalculated automatically and displayed. Press Enter and return to step 5. If you want to reallocate the budget, specify a different allocation curve and press Enter. The total is then reallocated according to the new curve and the new budget is displayed. Press Enter and return to step 5. If you do not want to make any changes, press F12=Cancel in (BUS101/B). Allocate a Budget Transaction over Days in One Budget Period In (BUS101/B), select option 11=‘Budget/Day’ for the budget transaction to start ‘Budget. Display Periodic Budget Values’ (BUS105/B). cards.goodAnswer: In (BUS105/B), select option Open for the appropriate budget period. On the E panel, the amount left to distribute (that is, the budget period amount) is displayed. Allocate this amount to the different days until the amount left to distribute is down to zero. Press Enter and F3. Return to step 5. Enter Budget Template This procedure is used to define the templates used by budget officers in decentralized budgeting. Each template contains a name and the budget transaction lines needed. Each line contains either an accounting ID or resource ID and period accounting curve. Example: • Budget template: Sales • Line 1: Domestic sales (with an accounting ID) • Line 2: Foreign sales (with an accounting ID). Before you start • Resources must be entered in ‘Budget Resource. Open’ (BUS015).

cards.goodAnswer: • Period accounting curves must be defined in ‘Period Accounting Curve. Open’ (CRS450). They are used to automatically allocate values to the selected periods. • You must know each budget officer’s budget needs; that is, what type of budget transactions the officer works with. Follow these steps Start ‘Budget Template. Open’ (BUS030/B). Check that the panel sequence is set to E1. Specify an ID to create a template and select option New. On the E panel, specify the template name. Press Enter to start ‘Budget Template. Open Lines’ (BUS031).

cards.goodAnswer: In (BUS031/B), specify a line number to create a line and select option Open. Return to step to create more lines. When finished, press F3. Enter Decentralized Budget This procedure is used by budget officers to enter a budget for the next budgeting period using predefined templates. These decentralized budgets can be made for each budget officer, budget number and budget version. M3 Business Controlling User Guide | Budgeting

cards.goodAnswer: A decentralized budget can be used to create a central budget. Before you start • A budget with basic data (budget number/budget version) must be defined in ‘Budget. Open’ (BUS035). • Costs must be entered for each resource. More than one cost can be specified for each resource. cards.goodAnswer: • Period accounting curves must be defined in ‘Period Accounting Curve. Open’ (CRS450). They are used to automatically allocate values to the selected periods. cards.goodAnswer: Before you start • A budget with budget transactions must be entered in ‘Budget. Open’ (BUS100). • Transfer template(s) must be created for the budget. cards.question: Page 27 in 'Budgeting' (Import from PC Document) - detail 2: Explain 'Import from PC Document This procedure' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.

cards.goodAnswer: Quick Entry - Budget This procedure is used to enter budget transactions in a more automated and faster procedure than the one used during normal entry. This is achieved by defining the design of the panel in advance so that only a minimum of data has to be manually entered for each transaction. Note that when using the quick entry method, you specify only the total amount and/or quantity and allocation curve to use for each budget transaction. That means you cannot review the allocated budget, as you can when using normal entry. Before you start • Your company must have well-defined routines for how to handle budgeting. • A budget including basic data must be defined in ‘Budget. Open’ (BUS100). Select option 12=‘Quick entry’ for the budget to reach ‘Budget. Quick Entry of Values’ (BUS102/B). Review the information on the E panel. cards.question: Page 30 in 'Cash Flow Management' (Cash Flow Conversion) - detail 1: Explain 'Chapter 2: Cash Flow Management Cash' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Chapter 2: Cash Flow Management Cash Flow Conversion This document explains how budget expenses and revenues posted on income or costs accounts are converted to payments or payments received allocated in time. You use these converted values in a cash flow budget for reviewing the expected cash flow. Outcome Selected expenses and revenues in a profit and loss budget are converted and allocated to specific periods and days. Converted budget values are posted according to user-defined accounting strings and saved in the following files: the basic budget definition file (FBUDEF), the file for budget details per period (FBUDET), and the file for budget entry per day/detail (FBUDAY). Use the converted values to create and update a cash flow budget – a balance budget – in 'Cash Flow Budget. Open' (CFS100).

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 30

cards.question: Page 30 in 'Cash Flow Management' (Cash Flow Conversion) - detail 2: Explain 'Before You Start • An account' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Before You Start • An account conversion table must be defined in 'Conversion Table. Open' (CFS010). • An allocation table must be defined in 'Allocation Table. Open' (CFS015). • Revenues and expenses must be budgeted in a profit and loss budget in 'Budget. Open' (BUS100).

cards.question: Page 30 in 'Cash Flow Management' (Cash Flow Conversion) - detail 3: Explain 'Purpose The purpose of cash flow' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Purpose The purpose of cash flow conversion is to display budgeted incomes and costs as future payments and payments received. This in order to predict an entity's cashes at a certain time in the future. When The conversion is performed each time you wish to create a new cash flow budget, that is, it is not a continuous process. When to create a cash flow budget is decided based on the entity's cash flow management routines and financial situation. An entity with solvency problems or a highly fluctuating cash flow balance is likely to make cash flow budgets more often, in order to control the situation and take required actions. M3 Business Controlling User Guide | Cash Flow Management

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 30

cards.question: Page 31 in 'Cash Flow Management' (Cash Flow Conversion) - detail 1: Explain 'How The conversion consists of three' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: How The conversion consists of three stages: Budget revenues and expenses are converted Values are allocated in time The cash flow budget is updated with the converted values. Stage 1: Budget revenues and expenses are converted Revenues and expenses in the selected profit and loss budget are converted to payments or payments received. The conversion is based on an accounting table defined in 'Conversion Table. Open' (CFS010). Which Values Are Converted? Which values to convert depends on the purpose of the cash flow budget. If you intend to use the budget as input for the expected payment lines in a cash flow plan, you only include revenues and expenses that are not registered in the sub-ledgers. (Totals for these values are automatically retrieved in separate lines in the cash flow plan.) Examples of values to convert when creating a cash flow budget for short-term planning are: • Revenues: Sales, financial incomes, dividend from share

cards.question: Page 31 in 'Cash Flow Management' (Cash Flow Conversion) - detail 2: Explain 'However, if you wish to use' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: However, if you wish to use the cash flow budget independently to review the long-term expected cash flow (for example, for all periods in the budget year), a more substantial part of the sub-ledger must be converted. Note: Only the posted values on income or cost accounts that need to be converted. Long-term changes in assets or liabilities can be included in a cash flow budget without former conversion. Examples of values to convert when creating a cash flow budget for long-term planning are: • Revenues: Budgeted sales and other incomes from customers • Expenses: Budgeted procurements and other costs. How an Accounts Conversion Table Functions Basically, the table consists of one or several lines. Each line represents a range of accounting strings in the profit and loss budget. Up to lines can be defined to make sure that all relevant accounts are included in the account conversion. The totals of budget transactions on these accountin

cards.question: Page 31 in 'Cash Flow Management' (Cash Flow Conversion) - detail 3: Explain 'This accounting string is then used' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: This accounting string is then used in the cash flow budget. To control that converted payments and payments received in the cash flow budget are included in the correct lines in the cash flow plan, for example, you need different accounts for these categories. Therefore, several tables are used for different sets of lines. For this purpose you can use budget accounts or the cash accounts used in the sub-ledgers. All tables created for the same accounting year are automatically included when converting the values. For detailed information on how to construct an account conversion table, see Create Account Conversion Table for Cash Flow Budgeting on page 37. cards.goodAnswer: Defining ranges and target accounting strings is a critical step in the cash flow conversion. Therefore, you need to have a thorough understanding of the company's chart of account. Using Account Conversion Levels Account conversion levels are used when a budgeted income or cost in a profit and loss budget affects the entity's cash flow on more than one occasion. The levels refer to the order of potential cash flows to be posted on separate accounting strings. You can define account conversion tables per level for each accounting year, from to 9.

cards.goodAnswer: Level always retrieves values from the original profit and loss accounting string. Level converts the results of conversion on level to a new accounting string, and so on. That is, all converted values on levels to are based on the previous level. In theory, all nine levels can be used. In practice, normally only levels to are used.

cards.question: Page 33 in 'Cash Flow Management' (Cash Flow Conversion) - detail 2: Explain 'Stage 2: Values are allocated in' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Stage 2: Values are allocated in time Simultaneously with the account conversion, the values are automatically allocated in time according to defined percentages. The allocation is based on an allocation table defined in 'Allocation Table. Open' (CFS015). This table also consists of up to lines. For each line you define the period in the profit and loss budget from which the allocation is made. You also define the period in the cash flow budget to which the allocation is made. Within the period up to five calendars day can be defined as payment days.

cards.goodAnswer: You then specify a percentage of the total payment to be allocated to each payment day. Note: The percentage must not be 100%. Sometimes you may want to allocate more or less than 100%, for example, to compensate for extra costs. Since you connect one allocation table per line in the account conversion table, you have great flexibility in defining the allocation. M3 Business Controlling User Guide | Cash Flow Management

cards.question: Page 36 in 'Cash Flow Management' (Cash Flow Management Tools) - detail 2: Explain 'Open' (CFS010) • A cash flow' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open' (CFS010) • A cash flow model must be created in 'Cash Flow Model. Open' (CFS020) Purpose The primary purpose of cash flow management is to ensure the entity's solvency and flexibility to conquer difficulties and take advantage of possibilities. An entity with a solid positive net cash flow can not be declared bankrupt. It has a greater chance of staying economically independent of customers, suppliers and credit institutions, and it can also compensate weak operative or financial result for a period of time. When There are no general rules as to when or how often you should manage cash flow. Whenever there is a need for a cash flow overview and planning the user can define his/her own cash flow budgets and cash flow plans according to his/her special business requirements. How Cash flow management consists of planning future payments and payments received on a short-term as well as long-term basis. Planning future payments receive

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 36

cards.question: Page 36 in 'Cash Flow Management' (Cash Flow Management Tools) - detail 3: Explain 'Normally, it is your customers that' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Normally, it is your customers that will contribute most to your future payments received. If a major customer has solvency problems, this can affect your payments received in an unpredicted way. Planning future payments is more reliable since it concerns your own costs and procurements. For this purpose, M3 provides two managing tools that outline causes for changes in the future cash balance: Cash flow budgets and cash flow plans. • Cash Flow Budget The cash flow budget retrieves and converts transactions from a profit and loss budget into cash flows. It is used both in long-term and short term-planning purposes, and contains values from different accounts depending on its purpose. M3 Business Controlling User Guide | Cash Flow Management

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 36

cards.question: Page 37 in 'Cash Flow Management' (Create Account Conversion Table for Cash Flow Budgeting) - detail 1: Explain 'A cash flow budget for long-term' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: A cash flow budget for long-term planning is more comprehensive than a cash flow budget for short-term purpose and is used independently. The long-term budget provides you with information which enables you to make long-term decisions. This could be issuing new shares to finance heavy future investments, for example. • Cash Flow Plan The cash flow plan displays the actual cash flows in the nearest future. This is possible since the majority of included transactions are already registered as customer orders, purchase orders, customer invoices, supplier invoices, service agreements, etc. The totaled values displayed in the cash flow plan are automatically retrieved from various M3 applications. The sub-ledger values, however, are retrieved directly from a short-term cash flow budget.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 37

cards.question: Page 37 in 'Cash Flow Management' (Create Account Conversion Table for Cash Flow Budgeting) - detail 2: Explain 'The plan provides you with information' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The plan provides you with information so that you can take short-term actions, for example, take a loan to cover temporary deficiency in the cash flow within the nearest future. Create Account Conversion Table for Cash Flow Budgeting This document explains how you create a table for selecting and reposting – converting – values from a profit & loss budget to a cash flow budget. Outcome An account conversion table is created for a specific accounting year. The table consists of a set of separately defined lines. Each line consists of a definition of a group of accounting strings in the profit & loss budget to be posted on a separate target accounting string in the cash flow budget. The table can be reviewed in ‘Conversion Table. Open’ (CFS010).

cards.goodAnswer: The account conversion table is automatically used when you create a cash flow budget in ’Cash Flow Budget. Create from Budget’ (CFS100). Income and cost values retrieved from the base profit & loss budget are converted into payments and payments received. The account conversion table is saved in the following files: Basic table data (FCFCHE) and detailed table data (FCFCFR). Before you start • An allocation table must be created in ‘Allocation Table. Open’ (CFS015). M3 Business Controlling User Guide | Cash Flow Management

Calculation of Cost of Capital - Cost Accounting The purpose of the process is to find rules for, and calculate cost of capital. Cost of capital is used in cost accounting to allocate costs of bound capital to the responsible departments/cost centers. This is done by internally calculating the cost of capital for a department's bound capital. This cost of capital will be a cost for each department but a revenue for the company. From an external point of view, these transactions do not affect the company's balance sheet or income statement.

cards.question: Page 57 in 'Cost Accounting' (Calculation of Cost of Capital - Cost Accounting) - detail 2: Explain 'A particular company has taken out' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: A particular company has taken out a loan to buy machinery. If this money had instead been placed in a bank account, the company would have earned interest on their capital. Instead, the company pays interest on the loan to the bank. For this reason, the company allocates the costs of the capital bound by machinery as a standard cost – cost of capital – for all cost centers concerned. Because this cost is written as corresponding interest income, the result becomes seen from outside the company. This example presupposes that the cost of capital is recorded on the cost center level.

cards.goodAnswer: This is not possible in some cases. If the company still wants to record cost of capital on its departments, the function is used together with allocations. All cost of capital is first recorded on a common cost center. The allocation function then divides up the common cost center's calculated cost of capital to each respective cost center. Purpose The control report contains all non-invoiced deliveries of goods or services based on customer orders, service orders and purchase orders up to a user-defined To accounting date. Contents For each delivery, the report contains the recorded amount, accounting date, customer, order number and order line number, delivery number, order type, item number, warehouse, facility and transaction quantity in the basic unit of measure. Source Transactions Non-invoiced deliveries are represented by the following account entries in M3: SO10- (for service orders), OI10- (for customer orders), and PP10- (for purchase orders). The basis for the SO10- account entries is created in the following situations: • Goods are reported as delivered to customers in 'Service Order. Complete Assignment' (SOS640). • An invoice proposal is approved

It is used for purchase orders that are connected to a customer order or service order with line type (Direct delivery). The basis for the PP10- account entries is created in the following situations: • Goods are reported as received in 'Purchase Order. Receive Goods' (PPS300) • Goods receipts are deleted in 'Purchase Order. Display Line Trans' (PPS330). The actual account entries are generated when you run 'Internal Account Entry.

cards.question: Page 61 in 'Cost Accounting' (Control Report for Delivered, Not Invoiced Goods or Services) - detail 3: Explain 'Create' (CAS950). The account entries are' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Create' (CAS950). The account entries are created in the table for internal account entries, CINACC. At the same time, M3 automatically populates the history table for delivered/not invoiced, CININD, with all account entries based on accounting rules SO10-951, OI10-951, and PP10-951. The information stored for each account entry in the history table is accounting number, sequence number, accounting event, and invoice matching date. As long as the delivery is not invoiced, the invoice matching date is 0. Selection of Deliveries When you print a report in (CAS515), M3 begins by selecting all records in the CININD table that have an invoice matching date of zero or a date after the To accounting date. A delivery is printed on the report when one of the following requirements is met: 1) the delivery has not been invoiced, and 2) the internal account entries have not yet been transferred to the general ledger. The CININD table is not updated

cards.question: Page 62 in 'Cost Accounting' (Cost Center Analysis) - detail 1: Explain 'If the delivery has been invoiced' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: If the delivery has been invoiced and the general ledger is updated, M3 searches for records with an invoice accounting date in the following three places: • Deliveries: Internal account entries in the CINACC table: OI10-951, SO10- and PP10- • Invoices: Records without an invoice number in the table for customer order headers, ODHEAD (for OI20-951), and service order headers, SDHEAD (for SO20-951) • Proof of delivery: Records in the CINACC table: OI15-951. Based on the dates found in these three places, M3 sets the invoice matching date for the respective transaction in the CININD table by picking the highest date of the invoice accounting date from these three sources. The invoice matching date acts as a tag in the CININD table that indicates that the transaction has been invoiced. A delivery is then printed as a line on the report if it has an invoice matching date after the To accounting date. Cost Center Analysis This process is use

cards.question: Page 68 in 'Cost Accounting' (Equipment Profitability) - detail 1: Explain 'Background The Equipment Profitability Model was' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Background The Equipment Profitability Model was developed for tracking revenues and costs over time for defined items of Equipment. The solution allows the user to first generate a predefined model with lines reflecting the business revenues and costs over the Equipment Lifecycle. In addition, other business objects (for example, a Customer) can be specified to reflect and analyze the same flows of Equipment Revenues and Costs. These business objects will then be stored against the detailed revenue and cost transactions in a time period based balance table. A maximum of predefined columns are available to deliver this together with the connected inquiry and analytical model. The Equipment Industries must be able to track all revenue and cost related to a single item of equipment over its entire lifecycle, which can span up to twenty years. Fixed/Variable Budget This process is used to create a combined fixed/variable budget with costs and revenues divided into fixed and variable parts. This is done by transferring values from one budget to two others that have been reserved for fixed and variable costs and revenues. These budgets are then used in cost center models and the report generator. A fixed/variable budget makes it easier to see how the outcome is affected by variable costs. Before you start • Budgets are defined in 'Budget.

cards.question: Page 70 in 'Cost Accounting' (Generate Internal Allocations) - detail 1: Explain 'Follow these steps Definition of Two' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Follow these steps Definition of Two New Budgets The two budgets to be used for fixed and variable costs respectively are defined in 'Budget. Create Fixed/Variable' (CAS160). This is done by first specifying the budget from which the values are to be retrieved and then by defining the budgets that will contain the fixed and variable costs respectively. Division into Fixed and Variable Costs In order to specify the costs, a so-called cost center model is used. This is a template consisting of a group of pre-defined lines controlling the type of costs to be included. The division into fixed and variable costs is determined by resource drivers. For each cost center account and resource driver, a variable share is defined as a percentage; the remaining share is seen as a fixed. This is done in 'Resource Driver. Enter Connection' (CAS041). The variable share is used to split the budget.

cards.question: Page 70 in 'Cost Accounting' (Generate Internal Allocations) - detail 2: Explain 'The division is done during the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The division is done during the budget process. Description The following example illustrates how the fixed/variable budget is used to check how the outcome is affected by variable costs. Budget A department has a budgeted cost of EUR 10,000. The fixed share of this amount – rent, etc. – amounts to EUR 8,000. The remaining 2, is a variable amount based on the assumption that 1, hours will be used at the cost of EUR per hour. The resource driver in this case is Hours. The outcome is EUR 9,800. In comparison to the budgeted EUR 10,000, the outcome is EUR lower than the budget. Adjusted Budget The conclusion that the actual outcome is better than the budgeted outcome presumes that the activity (in this case calculated in hours) has been the same.

docLinks.label: M3 Business Controlling User Guide_30072025_controlling.pdf - page 70

cards.question: Page 70 in 'Cost Accounting' (Generate Internal Allocations) - detail 3: Explain 'However, in this example, the activity' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: However, in this example, the activity has been decreased to which means the budget has to be adjusted. The fixed part is still EUR 8,000. The variable part of the adjusted budget is EUR 1, with the same cost per hour ( hours x EUR 2). The total cost will then be EUR 9,600. The cost outcome of EUR 9, demonstrates however that what seemingly was an improvement of EUR against the budget is in fact a deterioration of EUR after adjustment. Multiple Equipment Lives by Period or Division Abstract The purpose of this functionality is to support the process for Equipment Profitability Portal (EPP) including multiple equipment lives. Background An equipment can have one or several lives. When a major overhaul is done a new life can be started. To be able to record the life and the date when it happens, new fields are introduced in the serial number file. A new program is created where a new life can be started and it also includes a record of the previous life.

Warranty Fund Valuation This document explains how to manage warranty funds within financials. Warranty fund valuation applies to both standard warranties and extended warranties, and is used to obtain a stable flow of warranty costs during the warranty period. An amount is credited to the warranty fund when an equipment is sold. Then the warranty fund is consumed through claim maintenance orders. Extended warranties can be sold together with the equipment or at any time. The warranty fund should be zero at the end of the warranty period according to the warranty fund rules.

cards.goodAnswer: Important Parameters The following two parameters in 'Settings – Cost Accounting' (CAS900) determine the creation of some account entries when a goods receipt of a purchase order is reported: • Split PO price variance. If the check box is selected, separate account entries are created for price variances for item and charges, respectively. A price variance can occur due to factors such as a changed PO line price and changed charges. If you select the check box, price variances are posted using accounting rule PP10– for the item and PP10– for the charge. If you do not select the check box, any variance is posted as a total amount using accounting rule PP10–911. • Separate PO exchange rate gains/losses.

Automatically Entering Cost Rates for Costing Components This document describes how to automate the calculation of cost rates for most combinations of costing components and cost drivers, except subcontracting costs. Company policy determines how often to update cost rates. Some companies review their cost rates every year, others every period. Outcome Cost rates are defined for material overhead, operation costs, tool costs and other common costs based on a cost budget. The cost rates are applied when you calculate the costs of a product and display them in a costing model.

About historic actual cost As of version 14.1.2.0, M3 Business Engine enables you to calculate a historic actual cost for purchased and manufactured items in arrears, regardless of their inventory accounting method. You can also update the General Ledger with any variance between the current product cost and the historic actual cost. The historic actual cost represents the most accurate cost of the material or operation related to the product, since it is based on real costs incurred during the accounting period or the company-defined date range. cards.question: Page 97 in 'Product Costing' (Calculating historic actual costs) - detail 3: Explain 'With rolling average cost, M3 Business' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: With rolling average cost, M3 Business Engine uses the average cost at transaction date for each stock issue. With periodic average cost, the system calculates a cost for the period and then applies it to all stock issues within a user-defined date range. With the FIFO or the LIFO method, the cost to recalculate is either the invoice price or the acquisition cost for each specific item at each stock transaction. M3 Business Engine calculates the FIFO or LIFO cost by identifying the applicable stock receipts and multiplying the cost from each stock receipt by the quantity. If there are several stock receipts, M3 Business Engine accumulates the value - invoice price, if existing, otherwise the acquisition cost from the MITTRA table - with the quantity issued and then divides the total by the quantity to identify the acquisition cost. Transactions within the date range you defined for the proposal are updated with the historic actual cost and historic actual cost status 1, meaning that a cost has been calculated: MITTRA (Stock transactions), MWOTPS (Cumulative operation - transactions) and CRACTR (Various accounting transactions). This valuation type replaces report layout in 'Order Costing. Print' (CAS550), as of version 14.1.2. of M3 Business Engine. The output is report of all orders that are still open. When the report is being created, M3 Business Engine automatically checks the related order costing tables per reference order category to retrieve the accounting numbers that are required to accumulate the booked values. At the same time, the CPOHED table is being updated with the closing date for orders that were already closed, based on the accounting date in (CAS300). Then create a new proposal in (CAS170) but select valuation type = 'WIP valuation' instead. The WIP value is then updated with the historic actual cost for orders that are still open.

Calculation of Item Cost per Inventory Accounting Method This document explains how the cost of an item is calculated in M3 based on the inventory accounting method selected. The inventory accounting method represents a costing method. For example, inventory accounting method Standard cost corresponds to the costing method standard costing. To calculate a historic actual cost in arrears for purchased and manufactured items, see Calculating historic actual costs on page 96. Outcome • You must understand the principles for determining the item cost based on different costing methods and different scenarios. • You know where you can review the costs and the circumstances under which costs you can change manually. Standard cost (Method 1) Definition A standard cost is a production or operating cost of material and operations that needs to be carefully predetermined. A standard cost is set to reflect the expected cost of acquiring a product over a future number of periods and is therefore used as a target cost that may or may not be attained. The standard cost is compared to the actual cost in order to measure the performance of a given costing department or operation. Thus, out-of-control results are easier to identify for items and operations with a standard cost instead of an average cost or actual cost. Applicability Standard costs are appropriate for items manufactured often enough so that material and labor costs are well known through repetition.
